review: The view was good (and similar to one I had just seen from a hospital room), the food was good (restaurant week portions) but be careful with the service....the reservation requires a deposit which is kinda unusual but the worse part was our waiter decided to double his tip.  We had a gift card and told him to give himself a $40 tip but he decided to process it as $80 and then we were given the balance on our gift card.  Had the balance on our GC not be checked by me, he would have made $80. Luckily we caught it and now have a major sour taste for this place.  Someone fixed it for us but the waiter never can back to apologize...his tail must have been caught between his legs.  Buyer beware. Should have lowered his tip but I'm not like that.  Kinda wish I ddI tho.
